Easily a contender of blanc des blancs from any French producer. Minuscule hill in Veneto, it was a lovely visit to their cellar built out of WWI tunnels. White peaches, elegant minerality, banana, melon, young apricots, very light citrusy acidity, subtle bitterness, definitely dry but with a sweet note. Yes a bit of brioche.  Would choose this dude any day against non-pinot noir-dominant champagne

Hill Farmers Blues (Mark Knopfler) One of the best Prosecco’s I ever had! It’s got the pedigree: it’s a single vineyard, multi-award winning sparkling. But I didn’t know that when I tasted it: it’s rich on the nose, fruity and sweet, with some lemon jumping out of the acidity. Very good, Cartizze (now a synonym for Dry but it’s also the name of a hill that is in fact a 1.000-foot-high vineyard of 107 hectares of vines, owned by 140 growers, yes I did my homework).

De cor palha medio, aromas de pera e maçã amarela, algum melão fresco, massa de torta, limão e pedra calcaria. Acidez alta, mineralidade m/a, corpo medio perlage persistente, final m/p deixa discreto floral. Complexo, boa estrutura, delicioso!

BB & B Grand Tasting Aromas of citrus and stone fruit. Smooth with nice acidity. Hint of sweetness. Lean and fresh. Decent finish.

Extremely light color and subtle palate at first. Hints of pear and white currant. Some herbs and flavours are developing in time. Finish is very refreshing and crispy. Probably the best Prosecco tasted so far.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

This wine is all on the palate which is still developing with some extra weight making it almost voluptuous. A touch of yeast hits the sides of the tongue, rounding this out nicely!  It's tempting to rate this at 4.5 stars! 92 pts

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: WSET L3 S4 at 67PM w Matilda Hui DipWSET  Wine no. 10: Notes of flowers, grapes with a fine mousse. Much fruitier and florally in style compared to the traditional method wines.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Has a light yeast tone that I’m sure appeals to champagne drinkers. Lemon and lime minerality with a touch of honey sweetness. Mayyyybe even see a bit of loquat on the finish. Elegant and sublime.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: fresh and elegant Prosecco with notes of green apple, pear, and delicate floral tones. Lively bubbles, crisp acidity, and a clean, refreshing finish. A well-made and very enjoyable Superiore

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Aromas of apples, pears, peaches and a crisp morning frost. Crisp and clean in the mouth too. Tart apples trampling all over your tongue. Finishes with an airy-moussey feel. Outstanding for $10.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Fruity aroma of ripe apple and citrus fruit with notes of flowers. Fresh, dry and elegant on the finish. Pairs well with delicate and aromatic seafood dishes. Also a lovely balance to salty appetizers.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Maybe it's the setting, maybe the company however this prosecco went down way too fast. We really enjoyed it. Served ice cold by a pool one could smell the apples and also taste them. Slight fizz but not overpowering and I also felt a slight hint of oak at the end but could have just been my impression. Really enjoyed this.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Aromas of honeysuckle pear citrus peach lemon melon yeast, wild flowers and citrus fruits are followed by flavors of apple, apricot, lemon, lime, nectarine and white peach, as well as interesting mineral notes. If oaked and aged on lees, expect additional vanilla and buttery flavors.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Thin head of fine bubbles with a steady bead of same. Very clear parchment. A rich nose with burnt sugar notes and biscuits. Petulant attack, with a zippy acidity from midpalate to a long dry finish. A lash of Pomme fruit here. Fine petulance throughout and into the very dry finish. A lovely, playful sparkler.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Pale golden straw color. Tropical fruit on the nose with tart green apples, peach & melon, finishing with a lingering minerality. Stayed here in Crocetta del Montello - spectacular location in Valdobbiadene.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: We finally had a late Italian dinner, after visiting our new store in the evening to discuss how we can further improve its display to attract right audience.   Dry and refreshing, with a slight honey and brioche aroma. Enjoyed with beef carpaccio and prosciutto with melon. This beef carpaccio was a discovery of the day! So tender.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Decent drink..above average for the type. You’ll like it if you like prosecco.   Straw pale yellow colour.   Nose is unremarkable, maybe some citrus.   Nice fizz. Dry side. Acidity provides freshness. Some more lime accents than sweeter citrus. Background Apple.   Overall very acceptable Prosecco. Will drink again.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 3.9* Really nice and well ballanced Prosecco Valdobbiadene. Highly drinkable with refresing sensation instead of quite high suggar content. Medium amount of fine bubbles.  Nose: apple, pear, peach, lemon  Mouth: medium body, medium(+) acidity, a bit off-dry but with well ballanced suggar with acidity. Notes of apple, peach, pineapple, melon, honey. In the finish a bit of minerals and lemon zest.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: The winery villa sandi is  family owned and is  located in valdobbiadene,italy.It was founded in in 1920 by mario polegato.the present winery was acquired in 1970 and renovated to be  a modern top prosecco winery.they did much to popularise prosecco to the international market.rs 17g/l,ta 5 7g/l alc 11%.grape is glera. Taste..intense,depth,sweetish,great minerality tone with mild acidity Jkyeo..92 21 1.2026

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A refresh and floral Valdobbiadene Prosecco Glera 100% that exhibits peach, white flower and candied citrus notes. Sugar content fermentation is stopped by refrigeration in the tank. The tank method bubbles are formed around the edge of the glass, while traditional method bubbles constantly rise from the bottom of the glass. Pale yellow color. M+ nose/mouth of peach, pear, lime, passionfruit, herb, white flower,  Extra dry(17g/L), Light body, M+ acidity, Low alc(11%), M+ flavor, M finish, M complexity. Drink now.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Color: pale lemon  Nose: stone fruit, white flowers, green apples, orange blossom, medium intensity, light bodied, medium acidity  Palate: green apples, white flowers, stone fruit, medium + acidity, light bodied, medium intensity   Finish: short +, green apples, white stone fruit, white flowers

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Very pleasant extra dry and refreshing Prosecco with nice aromas of white flesh fruits, citrus, some minerals, peach, tropical fruits in a medium body and medium-long aftertaste sensation. Light, easy drinking with very good vfm, it matched perfectly with my talagani cheese .

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Villa Sandi is located in Friuli, not clear whether it’s blend or uva singola but definitely Glera dominant. Pale yellow colour, extra fine bubble, ripe, almost sweet apple, floral tones violet Excellent Prosecco

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Simply Italian Great Wines Americas Tour IEEM 2021 in San Francisco.    Glera w/ 12 g/L RS and 11% ABV.    Aroma of yeast, golden apples, and pears.  High acidity, minerality w/ flavors of bitter almonds, golden apples, citrus zest, and unripe fruit.    3.9⭐️

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Plenty of bubbles and lovely gold color. Notes of green fruit mostly apple and  pear and then some melon tingles on the palate. Really refreshing overall with a great body too. Found in number 21 off license for 16euro. Absolutely delighted with the purchase overall good 😊

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Great bubbly aperitif. It is dry, medium-to-light bodied, with elevated acidity. Color: pale straw yellow Nose: citrus, overripe apple and yeast medium intensity On the palate: pear, vanilla, pineapple Prolonged creamy finish with notes of peach and almond

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Villa Sandi Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry BD2024 (20250716) Pale lemon, medium intensity, lemon, juicy white peach, nectarine, honeydew, acacia, medium dry, medium+ acidity, medium alcohol, medium body with vivid mousse, medium finish, good, more promient & ripe fruity character than average Prosecco with nice balanced structure well-suited to festive consumption.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry

Aromas of peach and green apple with hints of ciabatta and butter. On the palate fresh with a zesty acidity, fruity notes of apricot and a really good and persistent perlage.  Fruity finish with notes of almond in the aftertaste.  This is one of the best Cartizze's I've ever tried!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Very good with a nice sweetness.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Am awesome Prossecco, light, we'll balanced, with hints if apple. Easy drinking

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Fresh, almost cider-like! Good for the wife!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Lemon and white peach with buttery notes.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Wow,  clean , crisp, good acidity, fresh

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Once again this was a standout prosecco.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Fruity & Chardonnay smells, more body, longer finished too.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: floral on nose hint of honey on palate.round and short finish.good value for money.like it.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: apple citrus honey apricot

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: apple minerals lemon tropical

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Very nice prosecco, tasty, balanced and refreshing. Good choice to start from.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Always reliable villa sandi. Silky floral, green pear flavours. Tiny bubbles. Creamy.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Tropical fruit flavors of guava, pineapple and mandarin orange are balanced by lively acidity, mixing with hints of apricot, nut and spice. This is lightly frothy, with a mouthwatering finish that echoes the fruit character.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A very clean style Prosecco, smooth in the mouth but with a quaint edge to it that is difficult to describe and adds an extra dimension. Very tempered with the acidity kept on a leash at just the right length. One of the best I’ve had in a while!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Surprisingly solid for a Prosecco. Brut and very close in style to a Cava or other traditional method sparklers. Good value.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: I really enjoyed this prosecco. The fruity aroma was mouth watering. Good acidity and off dry sweetness. This would pair really well with oysters.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Light golden color Prosecco. Tiny bubbles. Nose with apricot. In the mouth freshness through citrus and apricot flavors. Very light body 🍾🥂🇮🇹

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Winevision 3.8  Pale lemon color  Scent lemon apple ripe peach  Dry 1 Acidity 3.8 Alchole 11 Body 2 여운 중간  1차 향과 맛 위주.깔끔함 괜찮은 벨런스

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Good stuff bright crrisp & rerreshing for the money its very good. Perfect sparkling for Wedding toasts, holiday gatherings & all that jazz

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Nose: quince salt touch of melon   Taste: semi dry kiwi Granny Smith apple gentle lime not too effervescent   Colour: light sepia in the sunlight

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Finas burbujas, no mucho gas. Afrutado, manzana, notas de Brioche. Bueno. Salud  Fine bubbles, not too much gas. Fruity, manzana, brioche notes.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Green apple and citrus flavours. Did not get a true experience as we used it to make an Aperol spritzer. Very nice on a hit afternoon.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Lovely and bright with the well-rounded crispness of pear.  Lemon tartness, toasted walnut. Hints of clementine and apricot.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: fabulous dry prosecco, bouquet reveals fresh notes of ripe yellow apples and acacia, as well as delicate tones of honeysuckle. Fine citrus accents!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Apricot, pear, apple tart, hazelnut, chispy and clean. Very fruity and lively. Drinking at home, summer evening on the terrace.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Fruit forward, dry, lively with lots of bubbles. Yellow apple and pear, melon, nutty, and creamy and smooth finish with a bit of banana.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A delicious and fresh Prosecco! One that can be consumed on its own, bubbly and refreshing. Paired with cashew chicken, yellow curry and wontons.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Absolutely a special bottle of prosecco, seductive and very aromatic.  Tropical and peach aroma on nose. High intensity of bubbles. Lovely bottle.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Light and crisp, easy to drink, dry and not sweet. apple pear grapefruit citrus flavors make it a very nice, dry but well balanced Prosecco

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: The ratings on this are insane. Incredible value for money. It’s smooth with just the right amount of bubbles and honey with apple minerals

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Mama mia!!!  For a guy that does not like frizzy wines to rant about a frizzy one!  Just grab a bottle during a nice and warm summer eve...& enjoy 🥂

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Crushed green pears, white apple flesh and foliage aromas. Rounded fruit flavours with green pears and apples, soft white rose petals. Decent length.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Medium perlage. Fruity flavours of apple 🍏, gooseberries, a touch of peach 🍑 and citrus fruits, pleasant acidity, medium to long finish.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 88 pts, good value Prosecco. Had a slight aged/oxidative note to it for a Prosecco but it added a bit of extra character—might have sat in the shelf a while.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Crisp and fresh Prosecco. Citrus, apples and some pear & minerals.   Just below 4 stars from me. Around 3.9 points I would say. Nice value one that I could have again.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Birthday dinner celebrations! Citrus on the nose with pear and apple tasting notes. Very tasty, but might also have been the delicious meal and atmosphere.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Lovey bottle of bubbles. Lots of flavour, apple citrus lemon, very refreshing to drink.  Expensive (£20) enough back home but here in Italy it's a steal at €5.99

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Nothing special and unique. Smells a bit hay, basic acceptable taste of apple, peach and pear. A bit boring, but perlage is so good, so +0.3 to this prosecco.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Pale straw color. Yellow apple, green pear, white florals, light minerals, apple peels, lemon zest. Little fizz. Fresh & delightful. A bit sweet. Drink anytime.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: First feeling smooth Prosecco at the end different tones of taste a little bit hard to describe. But in all a good Prosecco. Pear and apple tones gently sparkling

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Pretty big on the almond and toast for a prosecco, and this came as a pleasant surprise. Add a bit of apple and citrus and it makes for a very versatile sparkling wine

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 87/100 Very elegant Prosecco, dry and refined, fresh and well-balanced with decent acidity.  Really enjoyed it!  Please see my review of 2020 for more details!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Very enjoyable.  Nose for apples, minerals and lemon zest.  Flavours of apple, lemon peel and asparagus.  Medium body. Very acidic. Light tannins. Very dry.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: I like this much more than I expected. I think my taste buds are changing again. Fruity, zesty Prosecco which I can have again and again. Good mid bodied notes of citrus, with a long lasting nose too

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Aromas of sweet pears, lemon, and grapefruit. Medium body, high acidity.  Palate displays a nice blend of citrus and sweetness, in a crisp and refreshing prosecco. Very nice, underrated

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Elegant palate with peach, apple and lemon. Complex body for its price. A trace of sweetness, but still refreshing. Stone and obvious honey (definitely honey!) in the aftertaste.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Pale yellow color. Green apple, yeast, floral on the nose. Very aromatic. Med body. Nice fresh acidity with some sweetness. Good finish length. Surprisingly good prosecco at this price.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: No wow factor on this wine, but still very tasty. A nice dry prosecco, I don't taste any strong notes of fruit/Apple like most prosecco. Nice to try and have once, but not great enough to buy twice.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Very refreshing although lacking a bit in character. Green apple, pear and grapefruit on the palette. Decent length in the finish.  Enjoyed at Pharos hotel, Hvar, Croatia

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A very nice prosecco, nice consistent bubbles that isn’t overly fizzy with aroma’s of ripe apple, tropical fruits and candy. Off-dry with a medium finish, not complex but can be enjoyed by most.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Excellent!  Love the flower essence but can’t define the actual flower. Crisp and fruity. I taste an apricot but not definite. Could not be more pleased. Can’t pinpoint but WE LOVE IT

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 3.5. Drinking at 7.30 am isn't wrong, is it? 😁 Breakfast with @[1|1723487|Daniel Giacinti] started with mimosas, about the best thing Prosecco is good for. But that wasn't all...

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Fresh, fruity Prosecco, with a fine, persistent perlage. Excellent as an aperitif. DOCG* Certified. Abundant in bubbles, it has a very fine, persistent perlage, accompanied by an intensely fruity aroma with clear hints of ripe, golden apple and acacia flowers.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Nice prosecco, very smooth. A bit earthy on the nose for me... some apple on the nose with some grass or soil. Interesting nose. But the taste is very fruity, some green apple and pear with a lemon and lime zest finish. I did enjoy it a lot!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Second bottle of Prosecco held by the lovely hostesses for our wine tasting, photo bombed by one of our servers, @[1|29379994|Chris Thiesson] #11 Nose: very fresh and bubbly with toast Citrus toasty buttery taste

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Straightforward as any good prosecco. Light on the entry, crisp & refreshing. Nose & palate, slightly herbaceous with hint of citrus & maybe guava. Mouthfeel, dry with rounded mouthfeel and great companion during breakfast

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Value And Perfect With Vietnamese Food.  Pale yellow gold colour with orchard fruit and lemon on the nose which continues to the palate.  Apples, pears and citrus with hints of tropical fruit.  The persistent fine bubbles with zesty acidity in a light medium body nicely complements the light spiciness of Vietnamese fare.  But I would not hesitate to serve as an aperitif or sip on the patio.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: I’m a 3.8 for this one so it gets a 4 ⭐️.  Nice golden colour in the glass, nice expression of bubbles with each sip.  On the nose citrus, apple and pear.  High acidity, light alcohol and a medium+ length finish.  Would definitely have again.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Everyday Elegance in a Bottle.A lovely Prosecco that captures the spirit of Veneto with its lively bubbles and fresh palate. It's an effervescent celebration of apple and pear notes, balanced with just the right touch of acidity to keep things interesting. This Il Fresco Brut is the perfect plus-one for any occasion, embodying the joy of the everyday in a glass.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 👃Silky, creamy fruits, with green and whites fruits, and an array of white floral and blossom notes on the aroma.   👅Soft, creamy with a hint of citrus, white pear, green apples and white floral flavours.   Gold Medal Winner in the Light & Fruity Category at The Glass of Bubbly Awards 2021

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: This bottle ended up in my fridge when we had guests over a few weeks ago. Didn’t end up drinking it then, so my wife opened it tonight.   On the nose, yeast, yellow apple, pear and honey.   On the palate, melon, apricot, apple, yeast and hints of citrus and tropical fruits.     Very nice.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: This was fun in a bottle.  The bottle needs to be double the size.  It goes fast.  Drop a strawberry or blackberry into the glass and Wow! There is a line of people with empty glasses begging for more.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Rapid review in the aftermath of Wine Fest!   Vivino community says 3.6, which baffles me. Another delicious prosecco from this winery. Dry, bright and so refreshing. Minerals, citrus, light yeast.   Enjoyed with @Shawn McLaughlin

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Strong nose of pear and yellow apple. Very flavorful sparkling wine with the continued presence of pear, apple and honey. Some acidity but not a lot. Medium finish with medium bubbles and effervescence. Perfect on a Saturday afternoon while watching F1.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Light colour, soft minerals at the nose. At the taste this prosecco is gently sparkling and easy drinking. Seems an extra dry with a touch of sugar, pear flavours and a balanced crisp flavour. Nothing special but not bad. At least the magnum bottle looks gorgeous.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Well enjoyed not by myself but by our guest Sanja at the excellent Oakville Thai restaurant Soontorn Banh Thai. Our go to Thai spot when in Oakville. Classy decor, wonderful atmosphere and superb reasonably priced food. As regards the Sparkler by all accounts quite good. Notes of apple , peach and pear and a nice wheaty palate offset with sparkling acidity. Very good pairing with the cuisine.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Light yellow color with a lot od small bubbles disappearing fast. In nose apples and pear fragrance with yeast scent. On palate decent acidity and clean orchard fruity taste. (*) Good to be drunk solo and mixed with Aperol.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: I am very very very far from considering myself an expert in the bubble world 😬😬😬  I likes it... and so did everyone on my birthday party... so everything is good.  The real wines we celebrated with were 10x and 20x more expensive... but the bubble part is not me...  at least for now.q

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: С Новым годом!!  Prosecco Treviso из региона Венетто.  Цвет чуть желтоватый, перляж крупный, вкус насыщенный.  Во вкусе яблоко, груша, цитрус, мёд, минералы и горчинка цедры в послевкусии. Отличное содержимое первого бокала в наступившем году!   Prosecco Treviso from the Venetto region.  The color is slightly yellowish, the pearlage is large, the taste is rich.  The taste has apple, pear, citrus, honey, minerals and a bitterness of peel in the aftertaste. Excellent contents of the first glass of the new year!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Surprisingly good considering that most entry level Prosecco is absolute shite and is not worth drinking. This stuff has a very fresh and elegant floral nose with a touch of cinnamon baked apple. Perfectly balanced acidity helps bring across flavours of exotic fruit to the palate to add to above mentioned aromas. Moderate sugar level, much lower than is usually the norm. Really refreshing and very pleasant. Probably the best frizzante I have come across apart from Valdobbiadene stuff. Happy days!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Great White Extra Dry Prosecco with very bright color. Luminous crystal with abundant lather and beautiful long-lasting fine bubbles forming even streaks. The clear neutral, mineral acidity, flint, grass after rain and fresh citrus.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Villa Sandi ll Fresco Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut BD2024 (20250716)  Pale lemon, medium intensity, lemon, yuzu peel, green apple, nectarine, acacia, off dry, medium+ acidity, medium alcohol, light body with vivid mousse, medium- finish, good-, refreshing Prosecco with good approachability due to fresh fruit character and 12g/L residual sugar.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: On the second attempt, the flavor profile is predominantly dominated by honey, resulting in a rich sweetness on the palate. However, there is a noticeable lack of freshness that could enhance the overall balance and complexity of the taste experience.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: We got ourselves a Magnum of this fun dry bubbly Prosecco, for the holiday lunch. Considering its price that’s a great bargain. There’s a nose of honey and peach with crisp, floral and strong green apple with slight minerality on the palate. Overall it’s refreshing and easy, perfect for any occasion.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Last evening on Corfu island - somewhat a dedication to Venetian pages of island history :) Meaning literally "The Fresh One", it's exactly the case!  Vibrant, fresh, tasty))  Very pale straw yellow color. Elegant & stable perlage. Beautiful bouquet of yeast, chalk, yellow apple, yellow plum, jasmine. well-ballanced palate of medium(-) body and vivid acidity. Delicately mineral. Flavors of peach, lemon & lime, green apple & melon. Harmonious fruity finish accenting citrus mostly. Was fine on its own. 3.6*

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Happy Bubbles Friday!  We started off our Friday evening with this 🇮🇹 Prosecco.  Prosecco seems to be slightly more comment type of sparkling around in 🇮🇩.  We guess you’ll be seeing more of that from us!  Light golden straw in the glass.  Meek lemon and citrus on the nose.   Lemon, nuttiness, and yeast on a crisp flavour profile.  This Prosecco does the trick for us!  3.6✨

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A fun, fruity and easy drinking Prosecco that is quite dry, well-balanced with a light body.  Medium size bubbles with a rich golden straw color.   This wine has intense flowery and fruity aromas pear, grapefruit and melon with a inkling of green apple.  It has clean crisp flavors of apples, peaches and pears with a hint of mint. Not as sweet as other Proseccos I have had it quite refreshing.  A nice wine to pair with a charcuterie and cheese platter.  Worth a try especially given the price point.  Enjoy!

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Cheap spumante for this quality. I think for a brut prosecco it'a a bit sweet, but the variety of notes, like green apple, almonds and honey, are sufficiently compensating that. The finish is long and quite intens. € 9,95@ Wine in black

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Loads of medium-sized bubbles in the glass. On the nose, apricot, citrus zest and pineapple with hints of honey and flowers. Dry, medium (++) acidity, medium alcohol. Kiwi, grapefruit and lemon peel with a hint of salinity on the palate. I was pleasantly surprised by the texture of the bubbles, but they're relatively short-lived.   For the value, this is a great prosecco if you're looking for a ripe fruit expression. Hints of tropical fruit, honey and citrus make this a great wine for a warm summers night.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: 3.7* 🚩Dress clarity: ⭐️⭐️ intensity: ⭐️⭐️⭐️ color: golden 🚩Nose intensity: ⭐️⭐️⭐️ duchess, pear, white flowers, citruses, lemon lollipops  🚩Palate sugar: ⭐️⭐️ alcohol: ⭐️ body: ⭐️⭐️ tannins: acidity: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ citruses & lollipops  aftertaste: ⭐️⭐️⭐️ . ⭐️ (min) — ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (max)

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: This was the perfect compliment with a bowl of strawberries. Add a few pieces to the flutes and sip to your hearts content. The bubbles made each mouthful a small celebration on the tongue. This was a perfect balance, not too sweet or too dry. Grab a bottle, someone special and celebrate your life.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: It stands alone strong as your reliable brunch Prosecco. Light, dry, citrusy. My go-to Prosecco for Aperol Spritz.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Creamy with notes of peach. Pleasant effervescence.   Tasted again 9/2/18 with notes of honey and green apple.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Apple, pear, citrus with a pronounced minerality that makes this a refreshing Prosecco.  Excellent at this affordable price

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Extra dry is the tipical Prosecco Valdobbiadene. Nice crispy fruit, fresh acidity.....very enjoyable.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: A really decent Prosecco 🇮🇹  Not a typical one, it has lots of melon, apple, lemon and some honey.  Cheers! 🥂🥂🥂

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Light and refreshing, this Prosecco is a great choice for an easy drinker on a summer afternoon. Not sickly sweet, but good balance.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Nice and mild Prosecco with a vanilla and peach finish. We had it at Vele with Estefania and her mom on June 2021.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Big tree fruit nose. Comes off pretty sweet but mellows as u drink it. A very nice italian sparkling. Enjoy

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Nice fresh perfume,Smooth, easy but complex enough. Nice small elegant bibbles. Even bettervthan the extra dry

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Good, honest Prossecco, pretty light, bubbly, taste of pears, citrics and green apples, not too fancy yet an enjoyable drink

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Loved it. This is a great every day easy drinking sparkling wine good fruit good effervescence clean finish.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Lovely golden apples and breadcrumbs. Wonderful roasted pine and a full, slightly floral madeleine finish.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: apple citrus pear green apple lemon grapefruit lime grass yellow apple green pear acacia gooseberry lime zest honeydew melon

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Tasted during Gambero Rosso Montréal 2023. No notes. Just rating. I'm keeping track of what I like or  not like.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Crisp, tidy, with uniform bubbles. Apple and green pear notes. Delicious with 1/2oz orange juice in my celebration mimosa 🎉

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: This is a excellent Prosecco very unique array of fruit, smooth and well balanced on the palate. Very enjoyable wine.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry
TESTO_COMMENTO: Back in the grind and literally have no time to write full reviews, so I'll just rate for the time being.

NOME_PRODOTTO: Il Fresco - Prosecco Doc Millesimato Extra Dry

4.3 Delightful. 15.5% ABV. Opaque ruby in the glass. Notes of ripe red plumb and baked cherries, roasted coffee and bitter cocoa. Aged 3 years in barrel prior to bottling. Extraordinary.
This is a classic Amarone. Lots of chocolate and black cherry, mixed with oak and spices.  Lucious wine that goes down smooth and velvety. Cheers!
Deep in color, powder Coco. Give me a straw cause this is delicious.  Bigger in body but well balanced.  4th from the valpo tasting class.
Wonderful flavors: cherry chocolate earthy.  Light tannins, velvety on the tongue. Paired well with smoked meats and steak.
Beautiful amarone in Viinimessut  with friends. Full and round, smooth chocolate, vanilla,plum and mocha. The best I tasted during the fun evening.
Look.  Up in the sky.  It’s a bird.  It’s a plane.  It’s Superjam!  Nice dry taste.  Awesome nose.  Astringent and pleasantly complex finish
Excellent & Typical Amarone Classico from Masi winery, Veneto, Italy 🇮🇹. cherry chocolate plum oak raisin leather on the palate. Great Amarone wine 🍷!!
Dark ruby red, on nose sweet cherry and dark chocolate. On palate great balance, nice sweetness. Getting some nice aging hints. Drank in a magnum. Really enjoyed this
Stone, mushroom, fig, jammy red fruit, coffee, toffee, caramel, smoke. Full bodied with ripe fruit and loads of auxiliary flavors with an outstandingly long finish.
Superb Amarone. For me, the best. Let’s say in Isaan, to pair with spicy dishes made with “moo” or “ped”… explosive notes of red fruits and sweet chocolate…
Me oh my!!  I love amarone.   And umm, currant and cranberry and plum and leather and a bit of evergreen and tree bark on the nose. the tannins are beautiful I’m in love omg.
Objectively and sentimentally one of my favourite wines. Blackberry and toast on the nose. Palate has a unique and delightful blistered tomato. Exceptional mouth feel. Exquisitely balanced.
Bomb. Leathery, earthy (plant soil??) base for dark fruit nose, pop of dark cherry and a little cocoa before a long, bone dry, oaky finish. Great color, burnt brown through a candy-apple red

Very balanced - perfume intensity matches palate - both moderate and the raisin is not loud, and the acidity just right - again, all balanced, not memorable (sounds worse than I intend it), but hits the mark for the entire crowd (-a pleaser).

2015 this time. Usual dark red with legs when swirling. Fragrance has plumb, red berries, and vanilla. Taste adds rich oak and chocolate while keeping berries and some plumb. Tannins are smooth and keeps a fresh crispy acidity. Can be had with anything but cheese is excellent.

Decanted 60min. The colour is deep ruby and very transparent with good surface reflection lights. The aroma start with blackberry, red berry, prunes, spice and a touch of forest floor at the edge. The second layer the chocolate, herbs arising. The notes is complicate. Very intense and full on the on the palates with smooth tannins, firm structure with fine texture mouth feel. It is has balance acidic, homogenous and dry with finishes savory dried blackcurrant with little oak and touch of coffee.

Family winery since 1772 named after their 1st vineyard "Vaio dei Masi". Dark ruby red colored. Seductive & delicate bouquet with glacé cherry aromas and notes of ripe dark fruit, herbs, oak, tobacco & leather. Similar to the bouquet, the palate is powerful yet elegant, slowly revealing its complex flavors. Cherry, plum, dried fruit, cocoa, coffee & spices like vanilla & cinnamon merge with silky smooth tannins and soft mouthwatering acidity in a long dry finish with a sweet touch. Rightfully called 'Gentle Giant'!

Deep ruby in color with aromas of ripe red fruit, oak and forest floor. Medium dryness, medium tannins, medium high acidity, medium alcohol with flavours of black currant, cassis, fig, jam, leather, vanilla. Medium finish. Very good.

Intensive and little rough in nose, in mouth much better, sweet and juicy on the beginning, spicy and little tannic in a few seconds. Plum, vanilla, chocolate, oak, pepper. Long, smooth, pleasant spicy finish. Very good, expressive, complex Amarone.

A dirty, gorgeous wine! Through their process, they dry the pumice and then re-ferment it. A color of vintageruby. A beautiful aroma of musk, smoke, black tea, chili, cocoa, coffee, and dried dark fruit. A flavor blend of red fruit, light spice notes and cloves, dark chocolate cherries, and oak notes. Wonderfully balanced.   Vintage - 2017 Tasted in wine class

A delightful taste.  Cocoa nibs, dried figs and plums.  The nose is haunting and it brings a silky texture to the palate.  This wine will be a capable dinner guest for any red proteins.  Plenty of structure but the tannins are fine grained and well balanced.

Absolutely fantastic Amarone. Smooth and juicy, big and bold, fragrant and elegant. Powerful stuff. Plum, raisins, figs, leather, cherry, caramel. Got better glass on glass and by the end was simply majestic.

Leather, tobacco, and earthy notes on the nose. A beautiful velvety chocolate taste, very subtle on the fruit notes but not withheld too much. Slight notes of dark fruit and plum.  I could feel the stress leaving my body with each relaxing sip. Surprisingly smooth. One of my favorites.

Ripe black-skinned fruit (mostlycherry with some ripe plum), flowers, and baking with a whiff of cedar. Concentrated but still elegant with smooth, structured flavors. The first taste is  prune marinated in spirits, followed by black cherry, licorice and cocoa framed in velvety tannins. Really a great Amarone for the price.

4,3/5 Wonderful! Ripe red fruit dominating on the nose with hints of orange crunchy and taste of chocolate, little bit of raisins and again red berries! Well balanced and very enjoyable. Well recommended!

2017 4.5 pts  I have been coravining this vintage to find a perfect pairing with Desi food. Plum, dark chocolate, and high alcohol content define this Amarone. I found a perfect match on this Eid-ul-Fitre. Spicy flavors of kachcha gosht mutton biryani elevated this already perfect bottle. Spice and high alcohol content don't usually go well. This paring was a divine exception!

Really good. Grapes were dehydrated 105 days before ferment, giving it a robust body. Very spice-forward, with anise, baking spices, pepper, black fruits. Tannic, medium acidity. Heavier on alcohol, perfect with pairing with something robust and spicy (paired with Mexican-inspired dishes at Alinea, poached squash with mole, etc)
Lovely smooth Italian red. Subtle notes of dark fruits, cherry, strawberry, pepper. Nice warmth and enjoying it on this Christmas Day!

A great example of an amarone, with everything dialled up to 11. Huge intensity, body, acidity, tannins and alcohol. Notes of cooked red cherry, red and black plum, liquorice, vanilla, leather and meaty.

This was an amazing wine to end the evening with! Medium ruby colour, aromas of black licorice, mushroom, tobacco. Good acidity and tannins. After some breathing in the glass, you get a full body with a long finish. Flavours were complex and included plum, leather, and cherry. Very smooth. Looses bit of its expressiveness after some time in the glass. Time to drink the 2011 year?

This is a winner.  It started with raisins the moment we opened it and the smooth layered tastes validated its specialness.  We enjoyed the fruit, the hint of valpolicello earth, and the quality construction of this wine.  As a fan of Amarone , this is one of the better wines — especially when factoring the moderate price point.  Oca Bianca, Santa Margherita Ligure 2025.  🇮🇹🏆
Plum, blackcurrant, pepper, ruby, light tannic. Never expect so impressive. Mid complexity. But can't breath too long, the taste will become lighter. plum pepper black fruit
Black ruby color black currant nosed good tannin spicy, earthness well balanced  good jammy nice wine.weaker than speri 2015

Really great. Full-bodied, smooth. Had at Christmas dinner and went well. Great to drink on its own. Paid $35 CAD. Would definitely buy again
Fantastic! Complex, rich aroma, ripe fruit and dark chocolate tones, super smooth finish. Classy stuff
Very good and flavourful   Dark chocolate and blackberry  Cheese, vanilla and various taste  Really good to enjoy.

A splendid well structured Amarone. 2 hour decant. Full bodied, juicy and nice balance. Nose of plum, cherry, chocolate and cedar. Flavors of plum, black cherry, prune, chocolate, tobacco, vanilla, anise and baking spice. Long warm finish of tobacco, raisins and baking spice.
Full bodied, jammy and bold. Deep ruby red color and extremely balanced.

Clean, medium to deep garnet appearance. At the nose, clean with medium intensity and flavors of red, black fruits and notes of oak. At the palate, dry, medium to high acidity, full body wine high in tannins. Aromas of red, black fruits with vanilla notes, long finish. Outstanding balanced wine with lenght, intensity and complexity.

Still good value appassimento. Overpriced at some restaurants but with the rich cherry, mellow tannins, cloves and oak finish, it’s a nice safe bet for most wine drinkers.

Pronounced with intense red fruit and black fruit of cherries and berries. Full bodied and very smooth. Highly tannic with a medium plus finish. £12.99 and fantastic for the price. Very drinkable

This is a really solid wine for the price, one of my go- to table wines. Can be had for under $11 at certain spots. Decent balance, tangy, red fruit, smooth finish with lingering soft red pepper. Fragrant nose with a hint of vanilla and maybe herbs and raisin. This is a consistent QPR winner.

A glowing ruby color with an amazing jolt of espresso aroma plus green tobacco, cinnamon, Mexican vanilla. The palate is well balanced with fine grained tannins, silky mouthfeel and a long chocolate finish. Cheers!

Espresso jumps out on the aromas, prunes, dark chocolate, green tobacco, vanilla, cinnamon. Palate baker’s chocolate, dark fruit, coffee, prune, leather. Velvety finish. Cheers my wine friends and have a wonderful day!🍷

Incredibly easy to make it disappear! Restrained and well balanced. What a cracking wine for the price.  Black cherry, blackcurrant, barbecue meat aromas. Dry with flavours of vanilla, oak, leather, blackberry, raisin, pepper and a long finish.

Vintage 2021 - Opened 2025. Beautiful clean medium ruby colour. Nose is full of red fruit, cherry, red currant, slight blueberry, oak, vanilla. On the palate it is very good, clean, primarily red fruit again, same as on the nose, with some dark fruit (blackberry, blueberry, jam) more in the background, wonderful creamy texture, oak, vanilla, light-medium body, soft-medium tannins, medium acidity, dry. Finish is medium, with a slightly bitter after-taste, with dried fruit, plum, jammy. Very good red wine from Masi.

Our sommelier offered me to do the pairing myself describing the dish. The dish  had sauteed Skrei (codd), beetroot puree, shortbread (zwezerik) and parsley. On the list was this Soave with some exotic tones in the description. The original pairing was an unoaked Chardonnay. To me Soave usually is close to a Chard. Paired side by side the Chard was fresher/cleansing the palate. The Soave was more complementing the dish leveraging the beetroot - on its own a bit heavy on the flower tones.
Classic style. Good white wine to have with sushi, sashimi, shellfish. Honey, Pear, white flower on the nose. Pear, citrus, honey on the palate.
👃: citrus, asparagus, minerals, grapefruit, pepper, cheese and stone 👅: apple, salt, grass, melon, peach, lime, tropical, banana and elderflower
A decent Soave. Not very deep or complex but well made , crisp and juicy. Lemon, apple, grapefruit  Medium body, high acidity, boozy finish
Nice aciditity. Pear and lemon scent. Good body with nice but a little short finish. Works very well with seafood. Good value!
Pale hay. Strawberry. Juniper and bit meaty, staw. Strawberry grape with flowery texture. Med body. Bit acid and savoury. Grape. Long persistence.
3.4 Honey nose, crispy taste
Anniversary dinner, great wine to go with it!  Light bodied, well balanced, but the nose is a bit shy. Palate shows citrus, pear, minerals. Nice finish
good Soave. Some acidity with lemon, pear and citrus fruits.  But this character not so strong and continue. you feel some taste of Chardonnay after some hours.
Very pleasant uncomplicated Soave. Apricot kernels on the nose, soft citrus (lemon and limes). A very pleasant summer drink, reasonable value at the price.
Trebbiano and Garganega from Masi. Meyer lemon, grapefrut, melon, bitter almond. Chilled just right and so refreshing. Cin Cin 🥂 happy Sunday V-peeps!
Same notes as '21 vintage. Perfect with light BBQ fare, had with falafel burgers today, was glad I picked up 2 bottles as it proves again to be an easy drinker.
Light, refreshing, with a green apple and guava note. It’s crisp and not too sweet, making it a great pick for those who prefer a fresh and zesty white wine.
Nice, fruity and fresh. Tasted good with today's starter which consisted of tuna puree in avocado. Tones of peach, grapefruit and lime. The wine is resonably priced
Yellow-pale color and medium plus acidity. Aromas of peach, lime, oregano and melon. On the palate, we can find grapefruit, melon with soft bitter touch. Good wine
An easy drinking neutral style white wine. Good aroma of pear and tangerine. Dry, medium plus acidity, medium body, medium alcohol. Nice to pair octopus.
Pale yellow in color. Pear, apple and citrus on the nose. Medium to high acidity. Light to medium body. Dry. Notes of more tree fruit and hints of citrus that lingers a bit. Good value.
Blend of Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave from Soave Classico region, Italy. Notes of wild flowers, apple, pineapple, pear and citrus. Light and fruity, easy drinking wine with medium plus acidity
Had this simple Soave with the fish antipasti. Very average wine, mostly aromas of citrus pear and green apple with a high acidity that helped clean the palate before the Amarone!
Chinese Dinner, steam fish, stir fry vegs.. Thought Masi would compliment well but its so so. Soft, mild acidity, lime , pear soft sweet but nothing amazing .. Just friendly
A quick holiday review. A light Soave, pale colored, citrus, grape fruit. Nice acidity, fresh. Enjoying in a fish restaurant in Borgarnes, Iceland. Price times 6 !, fish dish was priceless!
Okay Soave. Medium yellow. On the nose peach, lemon, grapefruit. On the palate medium bodied, medium acidity, saline. Medium length, dry finish. Drank at Karolyn Court. Bought from CentralWorld.
Pale lemon, apple pear medium intensity of blossoms, white each, lemon, wet stone, honey . It is youthful. Dry, high acidity, medium alcohol, medium minus body, medium intensity and medium finish

Simple, easy, quite thin Soave, which is much better on the nose than on the palate. Red apple, peach, orange blossom, touch of chamomile, some waxy, stony minerality. Unripe apricot, apple on the palate, med+ acidity, med- body, a bit empty on the mid-palate, but the aftertaste is quite nice. Garganega gives it's melon/honey character also, especially on the nose...

Pale gold with citrus-led aromatics and a hint of ripe stone fruit. Fresh and vibrant on the palate, driven by high acidity with a medium body that gives a bit of presence. Clean, straightforward, and refreshing with a smooth, moderate finish.

Insufficiently sour, that’s the main drawback of this Soave. Butter, pear, honeydew melon, minerality, lime, and a bit of petroleum, and salt. Yes, full, can say oily, and mineral. The lack of acids is more than disappointing in such well built wine.

WSET 3 The wine is a medium lemon color. The nose is of medium (+) intensity and has youthful aromas of peach, nectarine, lemon, chalk. There is some salinity on the palate, with medium acidity, medium alcohol, medium body and medium length. The quality is good. Can drink now, potential for further aging.

Pale lemon. Medium intense, pear, lemon, orange peel, peach, white flowers. Dry, medium acidity, medium alcohol and medium body, lemon, lime, white grapefruit, hint of mineral saltiness during medium finish. Just average good Soave Classico. Drink and forget.

Very nice crisp and fresh Soave. Classical notes.  Pear and peach, galia melon and a hint of grapefruit bitterness.  Well balanced acidity with a mild sweetness. Not as jaw breaking as some.  Very nice sipping.

Masi Levarie Soave Classico (12%) DOC  2021. Made with a Garganega grape. Tasting notes: flavors of stone fruits, citrus & pears. Lesser notes of pineapple & floralness. Sweetness is moderate low, acidity is nice & this wine has a long finish. We are giving this a 3.8/5 star rating. Bought from Sobeys, $19

Masi Levarie Soave Classico DOC 2023 (20241127) Pale lemon, medium intensity, green apple, lemon, white peach, linden blossom, fennel, dry, high acidity, medium alcohol, medium body, medium- finish, good-, fresh and fruity suitable for early consumption.

20210421 - Very subtle notes of garden herbs , perhaps parsley and chives   Light dry mouthfeel with delayed acidity and a hint of wood at the end  Picked it as a New World SauvBlanc  MC's @ Zoom Session Themed Whites

Green apple, peach, lemon and more citrus aromas with some grass, acacia flower, flint & honey hints. Even more of that on the palate but rather on the yellow apple and a bit salty side. Medium+ acidity, quite nice body as for young Soave, dry but with mouthful of tree fruits in the finish as well. Nice balance between fresh and ripe fruitiness.

Very floral, aromatic, generous whiffs of minerality  Citrus, lime peel, very balanced integrated acidity with minerality, white stone fruits, sweet green apple ripe pear. A trifle short in finishing but good racy acidity, light lifted perfumed thought surely a new world sauv blanc, X!  3.7☆ nice easy drink aromatic Soave! Trebbiano + Garganega. Not exquisite but great value, pairs very easily with most savoury food. 12.5%alc  LMC's TB Zoom exchange blind wine tasting 20.04.2021 Theme- White

Masi's classic white wine presents a fresh and balanced character. Light straw in color, shiny.  🍇Intense aromas of pear, citrus, and pineapple, with floral hints and delicate minerality.  🍇Dry, crisp, and light-medium body, with pleasant acidity and harmonious finish.  🍇Food pairing: Excellent as an aperitif, with fish dishes, light pastas, and salads.  🍇lovely and classic wine, suitable for young drinking, nice for the Summer.  Cheers ! 🍇🥂

Fruity ripe tropical nose with grass in the background. Dry, ripe mango notes, crisp green flavors and lighter acidity with a little citrus hanging in the background. Finish is relatively long with minerals. Best served  chilled. Extra half star for value.

What you want in soave, cool classic citrus freshness, crisp and light, lemony, perfect for summery seafood. Not quite enough intrigue here for me to give it four but it represents good value for money

3.6  👃🏻 Melon, asian pear, straw, grapefruit and stone...   👅 Pear, pineapple, minerals, tangerine and lemon grass...  An intruiguing Italian Soave made from 85% Garganega & 15% Trebbiano... Punjent tree fruits dominate the aromas, which open up to surprisingly juicy, fruity flavours, which do then leave a lasting tart aftertaste...  This wine is well balanced... The Trebbiano softens the otherwise very tart and dry Garganega... 🥂👌🏻
Pale yellow look. Aromas of minerals, stone,  earthy. In the palate you’ll find pear, apple. Nice for the price!
For the 2013, nose of apricots and flowers (maybe wisteria).  Smooth.  A hint of pear.  Great with salmon!
Good one but not a special one. Lemon lime honeysuckle pear Apple and a hint of mineral notes like flint
Subtle crips and refreshing Soave with a little buttery aftertaste. Notes of  green apple, pear and  citrus.
Very neutral in terms of flavor, lemon zest with just a hint of yellow pear. Good acidity, decent lenght.
My first wine from Garganega grape and I loved it. Nice, fresh, will definitely explore more wines from this variety!  citrus lemon pear

Intense aromas of ripe black cherries, with a herby edge of fresh sage.   This is Ripasso style - with a proportion of leftover skins from Amarone adding complexity in the form of leather, tobacco, and dark chocolate flavours.  This is beautifully structured with a medium+ body and a lovely balance of acidity and tannins. The tannins are surprisingly fine and lead to an elegant finish.  Paired well with the steak at a'Mare at the Crown in Sydney - Jan 2026.

RL = 3.8 & VV=4.0 🇮🇹🇮🇹🇮🇹 Corvina Veronese+Corvinone+ Rondinella+Sangiovesi (40/30/25/5)%  Alegrini's family is the leader of the Valpolicella Classic Style. The reasons are: more than 520 years dedicated to the vineyard development in it's more than 100 hectares and its different drying processes.  👁Dark Ruby w violet touch. 👃Tobacco & Raisins   💋Dried BlackBerry, black pepper, cherry, clove, vanilla w Subdued & smooth tannins  👚med+ 💫 Marked by its sweet fruit spectrum  ⏰Med.  permanence.
Very fruity with lot of Ripasso style. Red fresh berry and raisin plum raspberry vanilla and spices. Very good made and cheap.
An enduring favorite. So much of what is great about Amarone, but has what you want from a less soft Italian at the same time
oak cherry earthy blackberry allspice coriander  High alcohol, medium acid  Nice smooth finish.  Very enjoyable  Great qpr  Will buy again
Earthy with oak and tobacco notes. Bold with a dry finish. Lots of sediment on the last pour. Medium tannins and a deep ruby color.
Went great with the burger at One Flew South (ATL) nice red blend, slightly tart, blackberry, cherry, pretty smooth, medium body
Earthy and smoke hint of berry and brisk tannins. Pleasant and even better with food (rare for me personally). Delightful find
Sweet Strawberry, raisin, prune, light vanilla. Just a hint of sweetness from raisinated grapes. Medium+ acidity with a good finish.
Raisins, oak, balsamic and mushrooms. Interesting all the way with an old world aged feel. This will start a conversation! 2017
Ruby red with purple hues. Velvety texture with raisins, vanilla, black pepper, cloves. A chew of cigar. Great balance and complexity.
3.9 pts Taste nose : oak leather chocolate Color : purple bold Taste : almost sweet but not, a bit pepper landing, finish good tannic
Tart, and dry oakiness. Dusty and medium bodied, lowish tannins.  EDIT: opened up a bit the next day. Nice, rich cherry flavor, medium full body.
Med to high acidity with heavy legs.  Dried blackberries, kola, black pepper, black cherries with tannins being countered by the sugar. Makes for a decent bar offering.
Violet in the glass with a sweet aroma.  Cherry and plum on the palate with a nice peppery spice.  It was good with pasta and interesting on its own too
Medium tannins, licorice, grape tomatoes like tartness, some cherry and light tobacco, easy to drink and went really well with some Havarti cheese I had
2020 vintage Medium ruby color Cedar oak blackberry nose Dry oaky, plum, Medium-highhigh acidity, Medium-highhigh tannin finish Mariage with bulgogi
A full bodied Valpollicella classic. Plum and cherry explosion with cocoa and pepper on the end. Beautifully balanced expression of the corvina grape. This is a real winner!
Tasted at the CAL-ITAL Summer WineFest at the Golden Nugget Casino in Atlantic City, on Aug 29, 2015. A great wine festival presenting just a small pinch of California & Italian wines.
Nice deep purple Italian red. Musty blueberry blackberry nose. Still has all the caramel and raisin notes but they more muted. Medium plus body and acid. Solid tannins and an excellent wine!
I have to revise my previous opinion. This bottle is much better. Or I am much better.  Corvina coming through in spades. Finish remains medium long, but that dies not spoil the fun.
My wife and both love this wine. Concentrated but not overbearing, great with food or on its own. Lotta earth, almost barnyard, dark fruits and plum on the nose. One of the best under $20 wines IMO
Made of 100% Corvina (one of the main grapes in Amarone) from Venetian regional of Northern Italy.   Somewhat raisiny, some chocolate, earthy, with moderate body and tannins, very nice.
Huge wine, nice balanced tannins, and a great buy for the price! Red Fruit like Cherry, Raspberry, and raisin. Oak, Baking Spices, and Vanilla add texture and dynamics.

Unfortunately, I don’t have the vintage...why do Restaurants leave that off the menu?!?! But I’m drinking this at an Italian Restaurant inside Chicago’s O’Hare Airport. Smooth but dark and rich - baby Amarone fits the bill as it has all the hallmarks but is much more subtle and smooth.

Full body, high acid, high tannin, dry  Nose is blackberry, vanilla, cocoa  Palate is cherry, leather, cedar  Long finish, not the most complex, great food wine  Edit: changing this to a 4 after an hour of decanting. Becomes much more open with some nice spice on the nose. Well rounded. Great value at $20

Valpolicella always delivers. This Allegrini entry level wine was quite good. We were excited to find it on the French island of St Barths after a week of Bordeaux. Good aroma (gets better with time) of dried fruit, subtle raisin and butterscotch, and nice medium bodied wine.  Ideally paired with our Carbonara and Margherita pizza.  Wife rated this as 3.9…but I’m going with a 4.0.   La Casina, 2025, St Barths.

Best way to think of this is like a baby Amarone - 30% dried grapes and  70% fresh grapes.  A blend of 4 grapes from a single vineyard there’s lots of dried fruit - raspberry and cherry - and some vanilla.  The palate is much richer with more dark cherry, liquorice and cedar. It’s so smooth but you up want to pair this with food. Would go with a lot and went really well with risotto!  Bumped to 4 for the value for money!

Dry red wine made from a blend of grapes 70% Corvina, 25% Rondinella, 5% Sangiovese, grown in Valpolicella, in the Veneto region of Italy. Fermented using the ripasso method.  Aged in used French oak for 15 months.  Taste like, lovely rose petal aromas with dried black cherry, tobacco and grace notes of dried herbs and black pepper with a ripe, velvety texture. Very consistent and delicious, over delivers.

Super smooth. Light, fruity and very drinkable. I don't normally notice the aromas on pouring but the smell of cherry hit me instantly. Looks quite heavyweight on pouring yet light and fruity on the palette. Reviews mention all sorts of tastes going off but it's mainly cherry, vanilla and a hint of tobacco for me.   Overall very pleasant. What I call a "safe" wine. One to please many people's tastes. This won't be my last. Rating for a 2016.

A nose of cherry  smoke and oak make this eligible as a fantastic men's cologne, never mind a wine. Well balanced and potent, this is dry, fruity, and unmistakably Italian. Pair with strong cheese pasta dish for an authentic experience.

This punches above its weight class… $15 at Costco. Almost tastes like a baby amarone.. drank and while adding some to my tomato sauce for chicken parm. Great value and of course an excellent producer

Only had 1 glass in Vintage wine bar in Athens. Chosen by the sommelier who listened to my preferences and offered this. Light, fruity, smooth, very delicious! Paid €10 a glass so I'm glad to see it's highly rated

Complex and grippy, heavily blackcurrant scented on the nose and palate with raisin, balsamic, oak and chocolate coming in through the finish with green notes (?blackberry leaf ?), mouth puckering at first but eases into a rich and rewarding glass.

Hard to beat for the price. It has freshness and crunchy juicy fruit like cherry and pomegranate with some damp earthiness and vanilla blueberry muffins. Some pyrazines but very minor. Fine but grippy tannins.

Amazing with beef stew. So smooth, like velvet. Cherry, cola, chocolate, oak, vanilla, hint of soft ripe strawberry. Then there’s a lovely bitter orange note too. This is more prominent when without food. I love it. Bet it would age amazingly

Very nice surprise!! In overall as usual, it s the thing you expect the less that come with the most impressive surprises Very well balanced wine, nice acidity, lots of clove, dry fruits Will match many pasta dishes as well as meats

The nose opens with ripe red and black fruits, cherry and plum, alongside chocolate and black pepper. The palate mirrors those ripe fruits, enriched by chocolate, vanilla, coffee, subtle oak, black pepper and a touch of purple floral notes. The acidity is well balanced, with a gentle sweetness from the appassimento method adding depth. Good complexity, solid body.

Red ruby color, on the nose vanilla, black pepper and cloves and mostly raisined grapes !! On the palate it is velvety, with soft tannins low acidity and a long finish, one of the best wines I can find in Qatar.

Cherry oak on the nose, all dark fruit and a leather tone to it. A little earthy on the palatte. Great to pair with high protein foods. Age worthy, will improve with time. You have to try maybe you can buy again.   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *   *  Score Based on my New Point System Scale. (85-100 points)  Final Score - 90.5

A wine right on point for the money! Only 15.99 at total wine, this corvina grape is actually quite delicious! First time I bought it was 11/24. We opened it 2 days later on 11/26 to try along side a Sonoma Pinot Noir. This was definitely deeper and dark compares to the Pinot but it is still very much enjoyable! We liked it so much that we bought another bottle to enjoy on 11/28 with the honey ham for the day after Thanksgiving dinner.Notes of dark chocolate, earth, leather, dark berries, oak, tobacco, plum,&smoke

Smoky, garlic, on the nose truffle/cured meats, balanced with sweet and clear fruit like pomegranate and strawberry velvety and butter  No bite smooth finish easy to drink medium body  Sooo good with smoky food, black rice arancini at the Hilton

Earthy, woody aroma that's somewhat intense. A touch of stewed black fruits or raisin. Opens up on the palette with surprising acidity given the aroma. Really interesting, and tempting to drink the whole bottle
Deep flavor. High tannins but well balanced with a long finish. Not much in the nose. Not smokey. Sweet black fruits
Lighter in taste than it looks. Medium to dry but ends with a smooth finish. Hint of cherry. Great price. Consistently good wine.
Excellent. Wonderfully plummy nose. Berries, plums in the mouth. Light tannins. Silky looooong finish.
A delicious wine at a great value. Soft with notes of ripe fruit, oak, and a hint of spice. Ready to drink!
Really opened up since laste bottle, plum and exotic spice. Tannins really smoothed out with cherry and prune finish. Nice !
Deep ruby love. Boysenberry, blueberry and blackberry. Red currants. Tobacco. Mineral. Woody. Herbal. Dry. Acidic.
Great Italian blend. Goes perfect with homemade pizza tonight! I'm getting notes of dried cherries and blackberries
Cola-colored Italian intrigue. Good, quality red blend with bits of vanilla, oak, and earthy qualities.  Very good value and flavor!
Great balance in nose, leather, blackcurrant, pepper, oak, in palate long finish of drak fruits and burnt wood...excellent wine.

Excellent especially for price.  Complex with earthy tobacco notes, dark berry, spice, raisin and sweet tobacco.
cherry oak earthy

A: Medium garnet N: pronounced aromas of rich strawberry, cherry, dried raisin, cedar, clove, dried herbs, earthy with leather. Developing.  P: Dry, high acidity, and full body with high alcohol and tannin. Dried fruit, blackberry and leather dominate as pronounced intense palate carries to long finish.   Drink or keep.   BYL4I4C3

Appearance is deep ruby,  Nose has pronounced intensity, prune, dried cherry, raisin, violet, molasses, nutmeg, cinnamon, clove, vanilla, pepper.  Palate is dry, with high acidity, high soft tannins, high alcohol, full body, pronounced flavour intensity, chocolate, pepper, liquorice. Long finish. Outstanding. Can drink now but can age.

It is very full bodied for a red wine, and medium bodied for an Amarone. It is medium textured with dark sparkly ruby eyes. It has a firm attack with hints of cherry, fig , and mocha. The finish is long and complex, however it is not as rich as most Amarones. It is fruit forward and the hints of oak turn into a raspberry, caramel cheesecake. It is also rare for the acidity to stand in front of the sugars, but with this Amarone the tart palate is first.

In Alberobello at restorante I’l Poeta Contandino with chef Leonardo. Dark garnet red, bouquet of cherry and plum, flavors of dark fruits, cherry, cocoa, hazelnuts, and oaky lush tannins. Modern method Appasamiento wine at its very best. One must graduate to these marvelous wines!!!

Aroma of figs, port, and dark fruit. Just a tine of tawny color on the rim. Rich and spicy, this is a complex. Opens with deep raisin flavor, chocolate, smoke, and finishes dry. Mouthfeel is full. Went superb with traditional bolognese and homemade fettucine. I would drink this again. And again.
2016: powerful and wink, brings all the corvina force into the glass with a touch of elegance and sweetness that helps to lower the alcohol tones… still really bold

Incredible nose of raisins, balsamic scents, new leather, fresh mud, herbs, toasted vanilla...in palate a well balanced mix of dark fruits, raisins, worn oak..a delightful essence.

Classic Amarone blend with Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella and Oseleta. Vineyards with an average of 30 years. Ageing in new barriques for 18 months. Residual sugar of 4,5 g/l. Intense nose with aromas of matured-dried fruits and spices. Powerful but also some freshness to balance this quite too modern Amarone. 15/20.
Medium to deep red, complex and long finish with hint of sweetness. cherry plum raisin oak

Super smooth amarone. Nice, well-balanced tannins. Long finish. Black cherry, tobacco, some spice. Dark garnet color.  We had it with Greek food - balanced well with the lemon and acidity of the food. This is so smooth you could drink it with just about any food. Would also be great just by itself. Definitely saved to my favorites.

Allegrini Dinner Ristorante Armani.  Magnificent dinner in Ristorante Armani in April 2018 with Ms. Marilisa Allegrini to taste the allegrini wines, this was the star of the night, ruby in color with black fruit nose, smooth tannins with oak and spice on the palate. Memorable wine.
A serious wine. Blackcurrant, raisins, a gamey, mushroom aroma, lots of pepper, vanilla and cedar. A Big Wine.
Very deep colour, medium to full body. Fresh, piercing aromas of black cherry, and sweet spices on the nose.  Smooth and elegant tannins. One of the best wines I have tasted. 🍷 95/100
Still continues to be one of my favorite Amarone. Beautifully bold with a nice note of raisin and prune, paired with an oak and some chocolate. It pairs very well with steak dinner.
Drank with Mat and Aaron in Trattoria after meeting Caesars.    Highly rated but for me is thin and dies on the palate.   P.S Mat hated it.
This was a tanic wine that tasted of cherries. I would not have recognized it as an amarone because I didn't get any dried fruit on it.
Brilliant wine. Full bodied. Relatively smooth. Deep red in hue. Raspberry, plum, blackberry, chocolate, oak. Definitely worth a try.
One of the best Amarone at the price. Complex with a lot of depth. Strong blackberry on the nose with some earth. Beautiful and bold on palate.
lovely. this is the wine I had by the glass at an Italian restaurant near tate modern. frustratingly im not sure if it was this wine exactly.
Typical Amarone. Full body, high alcohol, acidity, pronounced intensity. Cherry, jam, forest floor, earth, chocolate. Long finish.
oak chocolate raisin blackberry prune smoke dried fruit blackcurrant
LA Puglia Las Angelas Woge. Practically perfect.

8/17/25 Vicino with Rob. This wine was perfectly paired with risotto. tobacco leather spicy with a dry finish.   Tasting Notes The controlled drying (appassimento) of the harvested grapes, which lasts about one hundred days, brings great aromatic intensity of dark fruit and undergrowth, together with spicy and tobacco notes. The impact on the palate is powerful and vigorous, with the wine gradually tapering on the acidic and savory momentum, extending gently with a clean, dry finish.
Wonderful amarone. It is so elegant, fruity and full body.

Dark purple in color with a barnyard, blueberry, and vanilla nose. Lots of wild red fruit in the taste, with chocolate, cola, cherry, spice, and earthy notes. “Wines We Ljke September 2024” wine tasting.

Amazing valpolicella! Deep ruby colour and tawnier towards the edge, one can find lots of preserved black fruits, raisins, morello cherries and chocolate on the nose. On the palate it is medium to full bodied with a complex smoky character. Well integrated alcohol and acidity levels.

2017. Medium ruby. A seductive smoky sweet earthy nose. Vanilla, mushrooms, wet earth and horse saddle.   A concentrated, sweet entrance into the mouth. Soaked currants in syrup, light sandalwood, cracked pepper and sweet spice kicking up in the end all the way up to the nostrils.   This is a near perfect for me. Paired with beef cheek.
Black currant grass herbal stewed cherries, plum marmalade vanilla nail polish, high acidity/tannin full body. Will be brilliant in a few years, very dominant tannins
Walkaround tasting so limited notes. Might have suffered from the tasting environment as my only notes are "service station toilet. Alcohol and tannin."
Strong, powerful, but very enjoyable texture. Need some time to open full potential but worth waiting. Beautiful match with red meats.

Nose: strong alcohol. Taste: leather, oak, ripe plum, cranberry. Raisin and Blackberry aftertaste. Smooth finish. Highly acid, dry tasting. Medium to full body.

Very different from what I am used to. Medium to medium full bodied. Tobacco, oak, plum, and a touch of earthy flavor hit the nose. Palate has cherry, red fruit, and a herbaceous note like dried basil. Finish had a bitterness to the likes of the aftertaste of a grapefruit. Grippy(ish) tannins. Delicious and different.

Very powerful. Very full bodied as expected from an Amarone. Nose of prune and dates and blackcurrant. A very distinct medicine nose and I got reminded of jagermister. Full bodied. So intense and powerful

Deep Ruby. Pronounced intensity of fresh and dried red fruit. Cherry, strawberry, prune, violets. Dry. Medium plus acidity, body, high alc,  pronounced intensity of fresh and dried fruit, cloves, violets. Medium plus ripe but firm Tannins. Medium plus Finish. Very good. Suitable for aging. Good concentration and mainly fresh fruit with strong structural elements like the very present tannins that will likely integrate better over time as well as further development of tertiary aromas.

4.2 *** this is quite delightful. Was a great compliment to NY style pizza with Chicago Italian sausage.  Deep ruby color with medium ruby rim and long, thin legs.  Nose of red fruit, dried leaves and brown sugar.  Opening of red fruit favoring cranberry with dark chocolate, brioche, fig and graphite.  Medium bodied and medium-plus acidity with an earthy, dusty tannin structure that lingers and fades slowly.  Peace and cheers!

The best amarone we had for this Italy trip. Bright Ruby red, what a bouquet on the nose! Very aromatic, floral notes of lavender, rose, berries on the palate, well balanced wine, honey notes, medium bodied, delicious with cheese.
